calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions To Trustees To Allocate And Distribute Trust Assets
Off Calendar Oct-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Steven Bovarnick for Nancie Garfinkel.
Kristofer Biorn and Timothy Watson (by telephone) for Cathy Baum Michael.

Counsel reported that they have revised an order that addresses Mr. Biorn's concerns with respect to funding of the sub-trusts and that it is approved as to form. The Court granted the Petition for Instructions to Trustees to Allocate and Distribute Trust Assets (subject to review of the revised order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Wu. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Bovarnick for (petitioner) Nancie Ann Garfinkel. Alicia Gamez for (respondent) Cathy Michaels.

Mr. Bovarnick reported that they mediated and have reached a settlement, that on July 6, 2016 they sold the last property, that there is an agreement on the tangible property, that there will be funding on the sub-trust, that they have identified Beth Kraemer to be trust counsel and that this case will need to be moved to Judge Cheng's calendar. Ms. Gamez stated that she is in agreement and that the request for legal services is premised on the idea of the petition being withdrawn. Mr. Bovarnick confirmed that they are withdrawing the petition and will engage Ms. Kraemer. The Court took the Petition for Instructions off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas Issued By Respondent Cathy Baum Michaels
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Continued To Feb-18-16 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions To Trustees
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Continued To Mar-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alicia Gamez for (respondent) Cathy Baum Michaels. Steve Bovarnick for (co-trustee) Nancie Garfinkel.

Mr. Bovarnick reported that he had had a jury trial which pushed back the discovery exchange, that he provided most of it at the end of December, that they met and conferred yesterday, that they are looking to mediate in late February and that the motion that is on the court's calendar has been continued. Ms. Gamez stated that the discovery that she has received has been heavily redacted, that they are still working on getting information and confirmed that all parties are available for mediation on February 25th & 26th. The Court stated that it will continue the petition for instructions to March 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, that they should know the results of the mediation at that time, that the petition can come off calendar if there is a settlement and that if settlement does not happen the parties are to have met and conferred as to how to resolve, what the issues are and a timetable.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Siu. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas Issued By Respondent Cathy Baum Michaels
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Continued To Dec-02-15 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions To Trustees
Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Continued To Jan-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Bovarnick for (co-trustee) Nancie Garfinkel who is present in court. Alicia Gamez for (respondent) Cathy Michaels.

Ms. Gamez stated that they were hoping to mediate in October, that opposing counsel has filed a Motion to Quash, that they have reached out to try to meet and confer, that she and opposing counsel have emailed back and forth, that they were hoping to meet in an informal manner and that she offered to agree to a protective order for the documents. Mr. Bovarnick stated that he came into the case in May, that mediation was set for July, that documents were provided but Ms. Gamez stated that they were insufficient, that Ms. Gamez has served subpoena's on banks and that he has a Motion to Quash set for November 18, 2015. Mr. Bovarnick further stated that they have filed a status report, that they need to protect the clients privacy, that they are trying to find a way to bridge the gap and have meaningful mediation, that they need a 90 day continuance to set a trial date, that during the 90 days they will do discovery and that they need deadlines. Ms. Gamez stated that mediation had been cancelled. The Court stated that it is hard to believe that they could not get to mediation, that there are ways to obtain information and that the parties seem more interested in fighting and dragging this out. Ms. Gamez stated that mediation is more effective when you have information. The Court stated that they are just spinning wheels here, that they are now imposing a motion on the court, that they need to get this done, get the information without involving the court and get to mediation. The petition for instructions was continued to January 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the case is not settled, a Joint Status Conference statement will need to be filed a week prior.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Court Reporter Katy Leonard, CSR #11599, 415-398-1889 (Reported)

Petition For Instructions To Trustees
Off Calendar Apr-01-2015 Continued To Jun-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Johns for and with (petitioner & co-trustee) Nancie Garfinkel. Alicia Gamez for (respondent & co-trustee) Cathy Bauer-Michels.

The court stated that oppositions and replies have been filed, that it modifies what was requested in the initial petition and whether the trustees can fund the sub trusts. Mr. Johns stated that there is a proposed plan of distribution, that one version precludes Nancie from receiving her distribution, that the focus is on potential financial difficulty, that the respondent refers to past financial difficulties and that the revision did not preclude distribution. Ms. Gamez stated that the issue at hand is whether the court can set aside the decision of a trustee, that there has been a pattern of financial difficulty and that her client has authority to post-pone distribution. The court confirmed that both sides are prepared to mediate, that they will need private mediation and stated that if mediation was not effective they are expected to have met and conferred regarding a schedule for further proceedings. The petition for instructions to trustees was continued to June 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to advise the Probate Examiner of mediation status no later than June 4, 2015.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR #13714, 415-433-5777 (Reported)
